questions.question: What is the structure of a transversal single-qubit gate U_L?

options.text: U applied to just the first qubit
U^⊗n — the same gate U applied to all n qubits in parallel
U applied sequentially to each qubit
A complex entangling operation across all qubits

questions.explanation: A transversal gate applies the same physical gate to each qubit of the code block in parallel: U_L = U^⊗n. This structure ensures that gates on different qubits are independent, preventing error propagation.

questions.question: Why are transversal gates automatically fault-tolerant?

options.text: They use error-correcting codes internally
Each qubit only interacts with its corresponding qubit, preventing error spread
They are faster than non-transversal gates
They only work on perfect qubits

questions.explanation: Transversal gates are fault-tolerant because qubit i only interacts with qubit i (in another block for two-qubit gates). An error on qubit i cannot spread to qubit j, ensuring at most one error per code block from a single fault.

questions.question: For the Steane [[7,1,3]] code, which gates are transversal?

options.text: Only X and Z
X, Z, H, S, and CNOT (full Clifford group)
All gates including T
Only CNOT

questions.explanation: The Steane [[7,1,3]] code supports transversal implementation of all Clifford gates: X, Y, Z, H, S, and CNOT. However, the T gate is not transversal and requires magic state injection.

questions.question: How does a transversal CNOT work between two code blocks?

options.text: One CNOT from block A to block B
CNOT from each qubit i in block A to qubit i in block B, for all i
All qubits in A control all qubits in B
Sequential CNOTs through all pairs

questions.explanation: Transversal CNOT applies physical CNOTs in parallel where qubit i of block A controls qubit i of block B. This maintains the property that each qubit only interacts with its corresponding partner.

questions.question: Under what condition is the Hadamard gate transversal for a CSS code?

options.text: The code must use at least 7 qubits
The code must be self-dual (X and Z stabilizers have the same structure)
The code must be a surface code
Hadamard is always transversal

questions.explanation: Hadamard is transversal for self-dual CSS codes where the X and Z stabilizers have the same structure. H^⊗n swaps X↔Z, so for self-duality, X stabilizers map to Z stabilizers which are also valid stabilizers.

questions.question: If an X error occurs on the control qubit i before a transversal CNOT, what happens?

options.text: The error spreads to all target qubits
The error affects only control qubit i and target qubit i
The error is automatically corrected
The entire computation fails

questions.explanation: An X error on control qubit i propagates through CNOT to target qubit i only. The result is one X error on each block (at position i), which is within the code's correction capacity.

questions.question: The Eastin-Knill theorem states that some stabilizer code must exist with a transversal universal gate set.
questions.explanation: False. The Eastin-Knill theorem states exactly the opposite: NO stabilizer code can have a transversal universal gate set. This is why techniques like magic state injection are necessary for universality.
